auth.appSubtitle: Personal AI coach with Garmin integration. Smart workouts straight to your watch.
auth.feature1: AI analyzes your workouts and suggests a plan
auth.feature2: Workouts are synced directly to your Garmin watch
auth.feature3: Running, cycling, swimming — including Ironman prep

dashboard.subtitleOnboarding: Complete a few steps to start training
dashboard.syncTooltip: Data syncs every 4 hours. Don't refresh too often — Garmin may temporarily block your account.
dashboard.stepProfileDesc: Specify sports, fitness level and goals
dashboard.stepWorkoutDesc: Ask the AI coach to build a plan
dashboard.noUpcoming: No scheduled workouts for the next 14 days
dashboard.confirmRefreshDesc: Don't refresh too often — Garmin may temporarily block your account. Data syncs automatically every 4 hours.

coach.title: AI Coach
coach.subtitle: Describe a workout or request a plan — AI will create it and sync to your watch
coach.clearConfirm: Clear all chat history? This cannot be undone.
coach.emptyChat: Describe a workout or request a training plan — AI will create structured workouts for your watch
coach.inputPlaceholder: Describe a workout or request a plan: "3-week plan for a 10K race"
coach.howToFindOnWatch: How to find the workout on your watch?
coach.garminSentSwim: Garmin: {{success}} sent, {{skipped}} swim workouts skipped (watch doesn't support)
coach.connectForReschedule: Connect Garmin or Intervals.icu to reschedule
coach.workoutNotFound: Workout not found in Garmin or Intervals.icu

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Account management and connections
settings.garminConnectionHint: Check your Garmin Connect email and password. Use the same credentials as on connect.garmin.com
settings.intervalsConnectionHint: Check your Athlete ID and API Key. Find them in Intervals.icu → Settings → Developer Settings
settings.garminPushHint: Try disconnecting and reconnecting Garmin in Settings
settings.intervalsDesc: Intervals.icu is a free platform for workout analysis. You can use it to send workouts to Zwift and other services. Works with Polar, Suunto, COROS, Huawei watches and anyone not using Garmin.
settings.intervalsStep4Copy: Copy Athlete ID (starts with "i") and API Key
settings.profileSubtitle: The more the coach knows about you, the better the recommendations
settings.watchSwimWarning: {{model}} does not support structured swim workouts with intervals. The AI coach will take this into account and create a compatible format.
settings.injuriesPlaceholder: Knee issues after long runs, Achilles problems...
settings.preferencesPlaceholder: Love intervals, dislike runs over 2 hours, train in the morning

workouts.title: Workouts
workouts.subtitle: Manage your created workouts
workouts.emptyDesc: Ask the AI coach to create a workout and it will appear here
workouts.deleteDesc: Workout "{{name}}" will be deleted. This cannot be undone.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.subtitle: Saved workouts for quick sync to your watch
favorites.emptyDesc: Create a workout in the AI coach chat and tap "Favorite" to save it
favorites.deleteDesc: Workout "{{name}}" will be removed from favorites.

bugReport.title: Report a bug
bugReport.description: Describe what went wrong. The message will be sent to the developer.
bugReport.placeholder: What happened? What did you expect?

onboarding.step1Desc: Go to Settings and link your Garmin Connect account so workouts sync directly to your watch.
onboarding.step2Desc: Specify your sport, fitness level, goals and watch model — the AI coach will give personalized recommendations.
onboarding.step3Desc: Tell the coach what you want: "5×1km intervals" or "easy 40-minute run" — it will create a structured workout.
onboarding.step4Desc: Tap "To Garmin" — the workout will appear in Garmin Connect and on your watch.
onboarding.step5Desc: Ask for a multi-week plan (e.g. "half-marathon plan for 8 weeks") — all workouts will be sent to your watch at once.

garminGuide.step1Desc: In Garmin Connect, open "Calendar" — the workout will be on the scheduled date.
garminGuide.step2Desc: A workout card will appear at the bottom — tap it for details.
garminGuide.step3Desc: All steps, heart rate zones and intervals are already set up.
garminGuide.step4Desc: Need to move it? Tell the AI: "reschedule my workout to Friday". The workout will be moved to the new date without duplicating.
garminGuide.step5Desc: Select an activity (e.g. "Run") — the watch will suggest the workout. Or: Workouts → My Workouts.

faq.title: FAQ / Guide
faq.subtitle: Answers to common questions and step-by-step instructions for GarminCoach AI
faq.whatIsDesc1: GarminCoach AI is a personal AI coach that helps create structured workouts and send them to your sports watch or Zwift.
faq.whatIsDesc2: You describe a workout in plain language (e.g. "40-minute run with 400m intervals"), and the AI generates a complete structured workout with warm-up, intervals and cool-down.
faq.whatIsDesc3: Workouts can be sent directly to your Garmin watch via Garmin Connect, or to Zwift / Polar / Suunto / COROS / Huawei via Intervals.icu integration.
faq.sportsRunning: Running — from easy jogs to complex interval workouts
faq.sportsCycling: Cycling — including indoor trainer and Zwift workouts
faq.sportsSwimming: Swimming — pool and open water workouts
faq.sportsTriathlon: Triathlon — preparation for triathlon and Ironman races
faq.howAITitle: How does the AI coach work?
faq.howAIDesc1: The AI coach analyzes your Garmin activity history over the last 30 days (minimum 10 workouts for sufficient data), considers your profile (fitness level, age, injuries, records) and engages in dialogue to find the optimal workout.
faq.howAIDesc2: The coach follows the 80/20 rule (80% easy zone, 20% intense) and may disagree if your request violates safe training principles: too rapid volume increase, daily intense sessions, unrealistic goals.
faq.singleWorkoutTitle: How do I create a single workout?
faq.singleWorkoutDesc: Go to AI Coach and describe a workout in plain language. Examples:
faq.singleWorkoutNote: The AI will create a structured workout with warm-up, main set and cool-down. You can immediately send it to Garmin or Intervals.icu, or save it to favorites.
faq.planTitle: How do I get a training plan?
faq.planDesc: Ask the AI to create a plan for several days or weeks. Example requests:
faq.planFeature1: All plan workouts with specific dates
faq.planFeature2: Structured steps for each workout (warm-up, intervals, cool-down)
faq.planFeature4: Option to send all workouts to Garmin or Intervals.icu in one click
faq.planFeature5: Option to save the entire plan to favorites
faq.planLimitsDesc: Maximum plan duration is 12 weeks. For longer preparation, we recommend creating plans in blocks (e.g. 4 weeks each) so the AI can adjust load based on your results.
faq.favoritesDesc1: When the AI creates a workout in chat, you can save it to Favorites by tapping the "Favorite" button. For training plans, you can save all workouts at once with "All to favorites".
faq.favoritesDesc2: Saved workouts are available in the Favorites section in the sidebar. From there, you can quickly re-send them to Garmin or Intervals.icu without asking the AI again. Unwanted workouts can be deleted.
faq.schedulingTitle: How is the workout date assigned?
faq.schedulingDesc: The AI automatically detects the date from your message. You can say:
faq.schedulingNote: For training plans, the AI automatically distributes workouts across days, considering rest days and proper load alternation.
faq.rescheduleTitle: How do I reschedule a workout?
faq.rescheduleDesc: If a workout is already scheduled in Garmin or Intervals.icu, you can ask the AI to move it to a different date right in the chat. Examples:
faq.rescheduleEx3: "Move the long run from Saturday to Sunday"
faq.rescheduleHowStep1: The AI sees your scheduled workout calendar in Garmin
faq.rescheduleHowStep2: Finds the workout and proposes a reschedule
faq.rescheduleHowStep4: The workout is automatically removed from the old date and placed on the new one
faq.rescheduleNote: Rescheduling works without duplication — the old entry is deleted, a new one is created. The workout will appear on the updated date after sync.
faq.garminConnectDesc1: Go to Settings and enter the email and password for your Garmin Connect account (the same one used on connect.garmin.com or in the Garmin Connect app).
faq.garminConnectDesc2: After connecting, you'll be able to see your recent activities and send workouts directly to your Garmin watch.
faq.garminBlockTitle: Garmin blocked my login. What do I do?
faq.garminBlockDesc: Garmin Connect is sensitive to frequent logins from third-party apps. If you see an error like "account temporarily blocked" or "too many attempts" — don't panic, it's a temporary block.
faq.garminBlockFix1: Wait 15-30 minutes — the block is lifted automatically
faq.garminBlockFix2: Go to connect.garmin.com in a browser and make sure you can log in
faq.garminBlockFix3: If Garmin asks for a captcha or email verification — complete it on the Garmin website
faq.garminBlockFix4: Then return to GarminCoach AI and reconnect in Settings
faq.garminBlockPrevent1: Don't tap "Connect" multiple times in a row
faq.garminBlockPrevent2: If the connection fails on the first try — wait a couple of minutes
faq.garminBlockPrevent3: Don't disconnect and reconnect Garmin too frequently
faq.garminPushTitle: How does a workout get to my Garmin watch?
faq.garminPushStep1: The workout is uploaded to your Garmin Connect
faq.garminPushStep2: Garmin Connect syncs it with the watch via Bluetooth (through the phone app) or Wi-Fi
faq.garminPushStep3: On the watch, the workout appears in the Workouts section
faq.garminPushNote: Make sure the Garmin Connect app on your phone is running and the watch is synced. Usually the workout appears on the watch within a few minutes.
faq.garminPushBulk: When sending a plan via "All to Garmin" — all workouts are sent sequentially and will appear in your Garmin calendar on the scheduled dates.
faq.garminSwimTitle: Why doesn't my swim workout sync to the watch?
faq.garminSwimDesc: Not all Garmin watches support structured swim workouts with intervals. The workout may appear in Garmin Connect but not sync to the watch (you'll see an incompatible device message).
faq.garminSwimSupported: Watches with full swim workout support:
faq.garminSwimFix: Solution: set your watch model in profile settings. The AI coach will automatically create a compatible format — with a text description instead of structured intervals.
faq.intervalsWhatTitle: What is Intervals.icu and why do I need it?
faq.intervalsWhatDesc1: Intervals.icu is a free platform for workout analysis and planning. It supports syncing workouts with many devices and services.
faq.intervalsWhatDesc2: Through Intervals.icu you can send workouts to:
faq.intervalsWhatNote: This is useful if you don't have Garmin, or if you want to train on Zwift.
faq.intervalsRegTitle: How do I register on Intervals.icu?
faq.intervalsRegNote: Registration and use of Intervals.icu is free.
faq.intervalsApiTitle: How do I get an Intervals.icu API key?
faq.intervalsApiDesc: You'll need an Athlete ID and API Key to connect:
faq.intervalsApiImportantDesc: If you've lost your API key, you can generate a new one in Developer Settings on Intervals.icu. The old key will stop working and you'll need to update it in GarminCoach AI settings.
faq.intervalsAthleteTitle: Where do I find my Athlete ID on Intervals.icu?
faq.intervalsAthleteDesc: You can find the Athlete ID in several ways:
faq.intervalsAthleteNote: The Athlete ID always starts with the letter i followed by numbers.
faq.intervalsZwiftTitle: How do I send a workout to Zwift via Intervals.icu?
faq.intervalsZwiftDesc: If you have auto-sync set up between Intervals.icu and Zwift, the workout may appear in Zwift automatically.
faq.securityGarminTitle: Is it safe to enter my Garmin password?
faq.securityGarminDesc1: Yes. Your Garmin Connect password is encrypted using AES-256-GCM — the same encryption standard used by banks and military organizations.
faq.securityGarminDesc2: The password is never stored in plain text, never shared with third parties, and never displayed in the interface. It is used solely for authenticating with Garmin Connect to fetch your activities and send workouts.
faq.securityIntervalsTitle: Is it safe to enter my Intervals.icu API key?
faq.securityIntervalsDesc1: Yes. The Intervals.icu API key is encrypted with the same AES-256-GCM algorithm as the Garmin password. The key is stored encrypted and used only for sending workouts to your Intervals.icu account.
faq.securityIntervalsDesc2: The Intervals.icu API key has limited permissions — it can only read and write workout data, but cannot change your password or delete your account. You can always revoke the key in Intervals.icu settings and generate a new one.
faq.dataStorageDesc1: All data (accounts, workouts, chat history) is stored on the server in encrypted form. Sensitive data (passwords, API keys) is additionally protected with AES-256-GCM encryption.
faq.dataStorageDesc2: Data is not shared with third parties, except for direct interactions with Garmin Connect and Intervals.icu at your request.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The requested page does not exist.
